item.title: Art Deco ceramic / earthenware jar
item.description:
This jar for biscuits or sweets, was probably made in the late 1920s / early 1930s.

Object properties
Material Ceramics
Design Unknown
Manufacturer Unknown
Period of creation 1920s
Dimensions (height, width, depth) 17cm, 20cm, 13cm

item.title: Everyday culture
item.description:
The main focus of my collection is on different everyday culture related artifacts from the 20th century. Since this is a quite broad area, here is a list of more particular topics:

Artifacts

  • Optical devices and toys
  • Consumer electronics
    • Tape recorders
    • Projectors
  • Hangers
  • Glasses
  • Airplane cutlery
  • Ceramic, glass and porcelain objects

Books and printed matter

(mainly between 1890 and 1945)

  • Advisory literature (especially with regard to transported role models)
    • Letter-writer’s guides
    • Entertainment
    • Health / Medicine
      • Personal hygiene
      • Sex education
      • Memory training
  • Beautiful children’s books
    • Scrap books
  • Photo books (especially in rotogravure printing)
  • Interior decoration
  • Atlases and maps
  • Advertising
  • Commercial art
    • Typography
  • Magazines
    • Entertainment
    • Fashion
    • Architecture
    • Technology

Art

  • Graphic arts and prints

Media

  • Tapes
  • Films (e.g. Super 8)
  • Slides / Photos / Postcards
  • 3D images / Viewmaster

My goal is not to cover any these topics even halfway complete, but to depict a (not necessarily representative) cross-section.

item.title: General
item.description:
• Freies Verlagshaus by Michel Graver
  • Kulturmaps Göttingen by Michel Graver
  • Nebeleule

item.title: Interessen
item.description:
I’m interested in various aspects of everyday culture in the 20th century and how it can be transported digitally. To do this, I collect various artifacts and also like to try them out. I also present some of the results and collections online.

My aim is to use up-to-date (i.e. digital) transport methods to support the immersion into the younger past, which has the most lasting effect on our everyday life today, the 20th century…

item.title: Amateur film about Marburg
item.description: A Amateur film about Marburg from the 1940ies.

item.title: Amateur film about Munich
item.description: The Amateur film about Munich is unfortunately not yet available online.

item.title: Art
item.description:
For my art specific projects (mainly Ric - an unknown artist) I would like to thank the following collectors, and recommend a visit to their websites.

  • Barina’s Dezente Hinweise by Wolfgang Barina
  • The Woman’s House by Gerbrand Caspers
  • Mark Strong from Meibohm Fine Art
  • The Linosaurus by Gerbrand Caspers

item.title: Projektemacher.org
item.description:
My “proving ground” to try out ideas and to present some of my collections since the corona pandemic is Projektemacher.org, there are various (sub-) projects linked. To a certain extent, these are old blog projects that were previously hosted on Tumblr. The newer part revolves around the presentation of various collections. These in turn can be divided into the following groups:

  • Resources for other pages (such as Schriftmuster and Vorsatzpapier)
  • Interesting content (such as Blaufusstölpel and Kinderbücher)
  • Experiments on presentation (such as Kleiderbügel and VintageReality), also have a look at the showcase below.

Continuous improvements to the existing blogs as well as some new ones are planed for the future…

item.title: Everyday culture
item.description:
• Facebook: Kleiderbügelmuseum Etlaswind by Matthias Dülp
  • Strukturwalzen.de by Tobias Ott

item.title: Life and work
item.description:
Places

I live and work mainly in Göttingen.

item.title: Postcards from Halle/Saale
item.description: Postcards of the sales outlets of the Gimritz estate.

item.title: Consulting
item.description: If you need advice on digitizing and presenting your collection online, I will be happy to offer my help and experience. Regardless whether it is a small collection, a very specific topic (e.g. to present an exhibition) or an entire library. This offer is aimed at all institutions of cultural heritage and other similar interested and is not limited to printed matter.

item.title: Digital images und HDR
item.description: • Greg Benz Photography

item.title: Professional focus
item.description:
Skills

My professional focus is on various topics from the spectrum of digital libraries and museums:

  • Full texts and metadata Blog posts

    • TEI / Digital editions
    • OCR
    • Search technologies (Solr / ElasticSearch)
    • XML technologies
    • IIIF Blog posts

  • (Mass-) Digitisation

    • Workflow automatisation

  • Digital images

    • Computer vision for the enhancement of cultural heritage Blog posts
    • Image processing and formats Blog posts

  • 3D Blog posts

  • Geodata Blog posts

  • Programming

    • Java
    • PHP
    • Python
    • Groovy
    • Go

  • Web development

item.title: Using materials and guidance
item.description: The source materials of my blogs as well as my printed materials can be used for your own work. Regardless of whether plan on doing a scientific work (master’s theses, doctoral theses, other publications), try to realise some creative idea (fonts, photos, etc.), exhibitions or your own web project (such as a blog, etc.), please do not hesitate to contact me. And if you are interested in one topic and still lack the right project, I will be happy to help as well.

item.title: Other
item.description:
Further links and without direct cooperation

Collections

  • Keramik Manufaktur Kupfermühle

Books

  • josef chladek on photobooks and books

item.title: Videos
item.description:
In the last few years I had the opportunity to be an extra in short films:

Evelyns Geschichte

External Content

External content is included at this point. Personal data might be transmitted to third-party platforms.

Allow external content

Here I play myself and accompany a very good friend with a serious illness to the flea market.

item.title: Blogs
item.description: A list of my blogs on specific topics can be found on Projektemacher.org. I am also involved in Nebeleule.de.

item.title: Colophon
item.description:
This page was generated with Hugo.

The theme hugo-video is used to integrate videos. Josefin Sans, League Spartan and Inconsolata are used as fonts.

The following JavaScript and CSS libraries and frameworks are used:

  • chroma.js
  • jQuery
  • js-cookie
  • Normalize.css
  • Enter-view.js
  • wordcloud2.js

Entries with 3D contents use:

  • A-FRAME
  • Three.js

The site is hosted by GitHub.

item.title: Contact
item.description:
This page is a project by Christian Mahnke.

Contact is possible via E-Mail.

item.title: “Seelischerkrankte Krankenhaus” (Mental health hospital)
item.description: I usually avoid buying reproductions…

item.title: IIIF Online Meeting 2024
item.description: I will present my IIIF HDR project at this year’s IIIF Online Meeting!

item.title: Climate change in art...
item.description: …is “old news” by now: Neither when it comes to the effects that can be recognised in old works, nor as an object of art itself.

item.title: Ultraviolet Photogrammetry
item.description: This article presents the world’s first HDR model of UV photogrammetry in a browser.

item.title: HDR IIIF
item.description: At Zeitzeug the owner’s wallpaper collection is being sold and I have secured a particularly bright example…

item.title: "Liebespaar" (Lovers) by Max Westhäuser (1885-1956)
item.description: I have acquired a colour woodcut by Max Westhäuser (1885-1956)…

item.title: JavaScript library for HDR
item.description: The JavaScript implementation for the last posts is now also available as NPM module.

item.title: TTS for "Briefsteller"
item.description: After the Briefsteller blog had existed as a skeleton since the beginning of 2021, I used my summer holiday to bring the sample letter, which brought me to the topic “letter writing guides” in the first place, online…

item.title: Siedelhof Jena by Curt Mücke (1885-1940)
item.description: I found an colourised etching by Curt Mücke (1885-1940)…

item.title: Contrast enhancement for UV images using HDR
item.description: This example is about a practical use case for HDR visualisation in the browser…

item.title: LibUltraHDR
item.description: After research, it was necessary to explore some possible workflows and to create tools for a project in preparation. Based on Greg Benz’s preliminary work on HDR support, LibUltraHDR was the most sensible candidate for further experimentation.

item.title: Sheet by Imao Keinen (1855-1924)
item.description: I bought a Japanese colour woodcut by Imao Keinen (1855-1924)…

item.title: HDR images in the browser
item.description: For a project in preparation I have compiled a list of links to HDR on the web or in the browser.

item.title: Data Containers
item.description: Even if it is not necessarily the intended use case, Docker makes it very easy to package large amounts of data with applications for simplified provisioning (such as APIs). This can be particularly helpful if, for example, standardisation data from different sources is to be merged for data enrichment.

item.title: Pong Visualisation
item.description: For some reason I came up with the idea of visualising Pong…

item.description: I found an etching by Kurt Mühlenhaupt (1921-2006)…

item.description: The latest blog incubated at the Projektemacher Labs has been started.

item.title: Reisen wie 1996
item.description: Sometimes you wonder how old the systems in trains are, here the passenger information system of an ICE 1 with an AMD 5x86 P-75 S CPU.

How to install a freshly built software from GitLab / GitHub?
On a Windows computer.
In an internal network - behind a VPN gateway

item.description: Kibana visualisation with room plans needs data, e.g. from devices in a building. But the data has to come from somewhere…

item.title: Maps for my Projektemacher blogs
item.description: Another addition from Projektemacher Labs:

item.title: Donation to the city archive Halle
item.description: Another donation to an archive should have arrived by now in city archives of Halle an der Saale. It concerns three postcards, the outlets of the Stadtgut Gimritz.

item.title: Christmas 2022
item.description: Merry Christmas to all readers, this year there is another present…

item.title: Exhibition "Wilma Bräuner"
item.description: From December 6, the exhibition “Spurensuche im Sylter Archiv - Zum Leben und Werk von Wilma Bräuner (1891 - 1985)”, on which Evelyn Kraßmann and I worked together with a team, will take place in the city gallery “Alte Post”, Stephanstraße 4, Sylt / OT Westerland. The web page is still under construction….

item.title: Hang pictures in Sweet Home 3D
item.description: For the planning of an exhibition using a 3D program, I have been looking into how pre-arranged picture frames could be imported into Sweet Home 3D…

item.title: Projektemacher Merch started
item.description: Even though some of the designs are older, the time has finally come:

item.title: Computergrafik
item.description: I had old computer graphics framed…

item.title: IIIF Proxy
item.description: To improve the ‘displayability’ of digital objects such as coins and books, I have written a proxy for IIIF resources…

item.title: Christmas 2021
item.description: Merry Christmas to all readers, this year there is a present…

item.title: IIIF Conference 2021: Scaling down...
item.description: Today I gave my talk about the infrastructure of my blogs at this year’s IIIF conference.

item.title: Instagram
item.description: As of today there is an additional Instagram channel. This is just an experiment for now. But what has already annoys me me: There doesn’t seem to be an official and stable API for creating posts / uploading images - very disappointing!

item.title: DIY Project: Phone case
item.description: Today, once again, one of the rather rare non-digital projects: A cover for my new phone.

item.title: Briefsteller.de started
item.description: Finally, the last of the blogs stared during the second Corona Lockdown is now online!

item.title: JPEG XL
item.description: Since my blogs are managed on GitHub, the images have to be stored as storage-saving as possible…

item.title: IIIF Konferenz 2021
item.description: I will present my Corona projects at this year’s IIIF conference!

item.title: Labs.projektemache.org gestartet
item.description: In order to have a page in the future that can easily break for a few days and to be able to present some things half-finished, there is now the Projektemacher Labs.

item.title: Living room renovated
item.description: In addition to my blogs, I used the corona period to renovate my living room. Here, too, I was able to realise some ideas that were years old:

item.title: Der erste Beitrag
item.description:
After the site Projektemacher.org went online in the last few months to unite my old Tumblr blogs and the projects of the first Corona Lockdown under a single

one roof as an umbrella brand, mine is now starting personal homepage.

A 60s Fat Lava vase.

Object properties
Material Ceramics
Design Unknown
Manufacturer Unknown
Period of creation 1960s
Dimensions (height, width, depth) 28cm, 23cm, 23cm

item.title: IIIF HDR
item.description:
This is currently a placeholder until there is canonical URI to identify this feature. It’s required to indicate HDR Content to a IIIF viewer.

For a simple demonstration of a HDR image resource see this blog post.

You can add the URL to this page in the supports section of the profile definition of your info.json:

"profile": [
  "http://iiif.io/api/image/2/level0.json",
  {
    "formats": [
      "jpg"
    ],
    "qualities": [
      "default"
    ],
    "supports": [
      "https://christianmahnke.de/iiif/hdr/"
    ]
  }
]

You can join the discussion on HDR for IIIF on GitHub.

item.title: Privacy policy
item.description:
Hosting

The hoster collects the following data transmitted by your browser in log files:

  • IP address
  • The address of the previously visited website (Referer [sic!] request header)
  • Date and time of the enquiry
  • Time zone difference to Greenwich Mean Time
  • Content of the request
  • HTTP status code
  • Amount of data transferred
  • Website from which the request originates
  • Information about the browser and operating system

This is necessary to display the website and to ensure stability and security. This corresponds to a legitimate interest within the meaning of Art. 6 para. 1 sentence 1 lit. f GDPR.
